summary: Reclusive American postmodernist author. Most commonly identified by The Crying of Lot 49 (Oedipa Maas, Trystero, muted post horn, Pierce Inverarity), Gravity's Rainbow ('A screaming comes across the sky,' V-2 rocket, Roger Mexico, Poisson distribution), and V. (Herbert Stencil, Benny Profane, Whole Sick Crew). Other works include Mason & Dixon, Vineland, Inherent Vice, and Against the Day. Known for appearing on The Simpsons with a paper bag over his head.

works.name: The Crying of Lot 49
works.description: Pynchon's short, accessible novel about Oedipa Maas, who is appointed executor of her ex-lover Pierce Inverarity's will and discovers a secret postal organization called the Trystero (or Tristero), symbolized by a muted post horn. Features the fictional corporation Yoyodyne, the Beatles-wannabe band The Paranoids, the stamp expert Genghis Cohen, and the disillusioned employee Stanley Koteks. Oedipa investigates the 'Greek Way' gay bar and learns about Inamorati Anonymous.

clues.clue: Oedipa Maas investigates Trystero/Tristero postal conspiracy; symbolized by muted post horn
examples: a character attends her ex-lover Pierce Inverarity's auction to investigate a postal organization symbolized by a muted horn, Trystero
Oedipa Maas' discovery of a centuries old secret underground postal delivery service called The Trystero

examples: Pynchon's job at Boeing inspired his depiction of a fictional defense contractor that appears in both V and The Crying of Lot 49

examples: a band of teenaged Beatles wannabes called The Paranoids appear in The Crying of Lot 49

examples: The stamp expert Genghis Cohen helps the protagonist examine the title collection

works.name: Gravity's Rainbow
works.description: Pynchon's encyclopedic masterwork, opening with the famous line 'A screaming comes across the sky.' The title refers to the arc-like trajectory of V-2 rockets. Features the statistician Roger Mexico and the Poisson distribution tracking a lieutenant's sexual encounters. Dedicated to Richard Farina, Pynchon's friend from Cornell who died in a motorcycle crash. Won the 1974 National Book Award. Edward Mendelson wrote 'Gravity's Encyclopedia.' Uses pigs to symbolize the 'preterite' (non-elect of Puritan theology), referencing ancestor William Pynchon's tract.

clues.clue: Opens 'A screaming comes across the sky'; title refers to V-2 rocket trajectory
examples: A novel by this author opens 'A screaming comes across the sky,' and its title alternatively refers to the structure of the plot or the arclike trajectory of a V-2 rocket

clues.clue: Roger Mexico, statistician; Poisson distribution of lieutenant's sexual encounters
examples: Roger Mexico investigates a Poisson distribution followed by the locations of a lieutenant's sexual encounters

examples: Farina is the dedicatee of this novel

examples: this other American author of Inherent Vice who wrote about Tyrone Slothrop in Gravity's Rainbow

examples: used pigs to symbolize the 'preterite' non-elect of Puritan theology in a novel that references his ancestor's tract

works.name: V.
works.description: Pynchon's debut novel, interleaving Herbert Stencil's search for the mysterious title figure V. with Benny Profane's aimless adventures with the Whole Sick Crew in 1950s New York. The Whole Sick Crew hunts alligators in the sewers. Benny calls himself a 'human yo-yo.' A ballerina dies impaled on a pole during a production with robot handmaidens. Kurt Mondaugen tells of witnessing the Herero uprising. Introduces Yoyodyne.

clues.clue: Herbert Stencil searches for title figure V.; Benny Profane and Whole Sick Crew
examples: Herbert Stencil and Benny Profane's search for the title woman
interleaves Benny Profane's meetings with the Whole Sick Crew with Herbert Stencil's search

examples: The Whole Sick Crew hunts alligators in the New York sewers

examples: In Pynchon's V, Benny Profane calls himself a human one of these objects

works.name: Other Works and General
works.description: Additional works include Mason & Dixon (polymath 'Learned English Dog,' Reverend Cherrycoke, pygmies living eleven days in past), Vineland (TV-obsessed 'Thanatoids,' Zoyd Wheeler jumps through window), Inherent Vice (Doc Sportello, Detective 'Bigfoot' Bjornsen, Coy Harlingen, Shasta), Against the Day (quaternion feud between vectorists and quaternionists), Bleeding Edge (Maxine Tarnow, post-9/11), and the short story 'Entropy' (Meatball Mulligan's party). Slow Learner introduction admits early stories had racist/sexist talk. Known for reclusiveness; appeared on The Simpsons with bag over head, punning 'The Frying of Latke 49.'

clues.clue: Reclusive; appeared on Simpsons with paper bag over head; food puns ('Frying of Latke 49')
examples: this author appeared with a bag over his head and made several food-based puns based on his work

comprehensive_summary:
Thomas Pynchon is the quintessential postmodernist novelist in quizbowl, appearing across all difficulty levels. The Crying of Lot 49 is his most frequently clued work as a giveaway identifier: Oedipa Maas is appointed executor of Pierce Inverarity's will and uncovers the secret postal conspiracy of the Trystero, symbolized by a muted post horn. The novel features Yoyodyne corporation (inspired by Pynchon's Boeing job), the Beatles-wannabe Paranoids, and stamp expert Genghis Cohen.

Gravity's Rainbow is the most prestigious work, identified by its opening line 'A screaming comes across the sky' and the V-2 rocket trajectory. The statistician Roger Mexico and the Poisson distribution are standard mid-level clues. The novel is dedicated to Richard Farina, Pynchon's Cornell friend who died in a motorcycle crash. V. is identified through Herbert Stencil and Benny Profane's Whole Sick Crew, who hunt alligators in New York sewers.

Pynchon's reclusiveness is a defining biographical trait, most memorably expressed through his Simpsons appearances with a paper bag over his head, making puns like 'The Frying of Latke 49.' Other works that appear include Mason & Dixon (Reverend Cherrycoke, Learned English Dog), Vineland (TV-obsessed Thanatoids), Inherent Vice (Doc Sportello), and the short story 'Entropy.' The entropy theme connects Pynchon to William Gaddis and Donald Barthelme. Against the Day features a quaternion feud. The John Batchelor conspiracy theory claiming Pynchon was secretly J.D. Salinger, to which Pynchon responded 'Not bad. Keep trying,' is a recurring power clue.

Novel: statistician Roger Mexico investigates a Poisson distribution of a lieutenant's sexual encounters
Novel: Herbert Stencil searches for the mysterious title figure while Benny Profane's Whole Sick Crew hunts alligators in New York sewers
Author: appeared on The Simpsons with a paper bag over his head, making food puns like 'The Frying of Latke 49'
Short Story: Meatball Mulligan hosts a multi-day lease-breaking party in this author's take on thermodynamic disorder
Novel: dedicated to Richard Farina, who died in a motorcycle crash two days after publishing Been Down So Long It Looks Like Up to Me
Novel: fictional defense contractor Yoyodyne, inspired by this author's time at Boeing, appears in both V. and another novel
